title: 11 Receiving ATM Traces and Adjustments
body_text:
11	Receiving ATM Traces and Adjustments
11.1	Method of Receiving Adjustments and Traces
The way you receive an ATM adjustment depends on the type of transaction being adjusted and whether the requester is the acquirer or the issuer. See the table below for details.

11.2	Handling THE EXCHANGE Network Advice Form â Credit Adjustment
If the initiator of an adjustment used the EXCHANGE Network Advice Form to initiate a credit adjustment, your financial institution will receive the adjustment via AFT.
Process the credit adjustment to your customerâs account.
11.3	Handling THE EXCHANGE Network Advice Form â Debit Adjustment
If the initiator of an adjustment used the EXCHANGE Network Advice Form to initiate a debit adjustment, the debit is processed automatically through the clearing system and your financial institutionâs Central 1 account is debited. The debit will appear on the CLI â In-Province Clearings â Credit Union Clearing List or the CLO â Out-of-Province Credit Union Clearing List. Your financial institution receives the EXCHANGE Network Advice Form, specifying the reason for the adjustment, along with other clearing items.
Process the debit adjustment to your customerâs account. If the other party is returning an unacceptable cheque to your financial institution (e.g., a post-dated CAD cheque or a foreign funds cheque), handle the cheque according to your internal procedures.
11.4	Handling the ACCULINK Service Advice Form â Debit Adjustment
If the initiator of an adjustment used an ACCULINK Service Advice Form, the AFT debit is processed and your financial institutionâs account with Central 1 is debited. Your financial institution receives the ACCULINK Service Advice Form, specifying the reason for the adjustment, along with other clearing items.
Process the debit adjustment to your customerâs account. If the other party is returning an unacceptable cheque to your financial institution (e.g., a post-dated CAD cheque or a foreign funds cheque), handle the cheque according to your internal procedures.
11.5	Handling the ACCULINK Service Advice Form â Credit Adjustment
If the initiator of an adjustment used an ACCULINK Service Advice Form, your financial institution will receive an associated AFT credit. Process the credit adjustment to your customerâs account.
11.6	Handling Traces for all Networks Except THE EXCHANGE
If the other party in the transaction initiates a trace, Central 1 emails the Request for Information form to your financial institution. You must respond to the request such that the initiator receives a copy of the reply within ten business days of the date on the Request for Information. If the request is marked as a FINTRAC inquiry, the Request for Information must be returned within five business days. For further information, see the applicable network operating rules.
Complete the bottom section of the Request for Information, explaining how the transaction was handled. Include your name and the date.
Return the form to Central 1. If your financial institution is the acquirer, attach a copy of the ATM journal tape to the reply.
If an adjustment is warranted, Central 1 processes an adjustment to your financial institutionâs current account upon receipt of the completed Request for Information.
11.7	Handling Traces for THE EXCHANGE
When the other party initiates a trace, they will send the Request for Information form to your financial institution. You must respond to the request so that the initiator receives a reply within ten business days of the date on the Request for Information. If the request is marked as a FINTRAC inquiry, the Request for Information must be returned within five business days. For further information, see the Canadian Exchange Network Operating Rules.
11.8	Handling Allpoint Adjustments
If the other party in an Allpoint transaction requests a trace or adjustment, Everlink Payment Services Inc. receives the initial request and investigates. If an adjustment is required, Everlink Payment Services Inc. forwards the adjustment request and supporting documentation to Central 1. Central 1 processes the adjustment and sends your financial institution the adjustment advice and supporting documentation. When you receive the adjustment advice, debit or credit your customer.
END
